case_title: JAMES HENRY REINHART VS. CALIFORNIA DEPT. OF MOTOR VEHICLES et al
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: ANSWER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION FILED BY RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA DEPT. OF MOTOR VEHICLES VALVERDE, GEORGE DIRECTOR DEPT. OF MOTOR VEHICLES

LAW AND MOTION 301, PETITIONER JAMES REINHART'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE AND REQUEST FOR STAY IS DENIED. A FAIR READING OF THE RECORD IS THAT THE EXHIBITS WERE RECEIVED INTO EVIDENCE; THAT THE PARTIES TREATED THEM AS BEING IN EVIDENCE AND THAT NO TIMELY OBJECTION WAS MADE TO THE HEARING OFFICER CONSIDERING THEM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR 8189

RULING - A R G U E D ; THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - PETITIONER JAMES REINHART'S Petition For Writ of Mandate and Request for Stay IS DENIED. A FAIR READING OF THE RECORD IS THAT THE EXHIBITS WERE RECEIVED INTO EVIDENCE; THAT THE PARTIES TREATED THEM AS BEING IN EVIDENCE AND THAT NO TIMELY OBJECTION WAS MADE TO THE HEARING OFFICER CONSIDERING THEM.
